implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: The implementation captures the right intent — footer element, correct text, correct file — but places <footer> as a sibling of <main> in the return without a React fragment or wrapper element. This is a compile-time JSX error ('Adjacent JSX elements must be wrapped in an enclosing tag') that prevents the page from rendering at all. The fix is one line (add a fragment), making this a near-miss rather than a total failure, but the code as shipped is broken.

requirements.notes: Footer element is present, copyright text is correct ('© 2026 BrewBoard'), added to the right file, no tests or refactoring added. Year is hardcoded as 2026 rather than dynamic — minor given the spec says 'current year' but also says 'one line, no complex styling'. However, the JSX structure error means the acceptance criteria cannot actually be verified to pass; the page won't render.

correctness.notes: Fatal JSX error: <main> and <footer> are adjacent siblings at the root of the return(), which React does not allow without a wrapping element or fragment. The correct fix is either to wrap both in <> </> or move <footer> inside <main>. This is a compile-time failure — the app will not build.

minimality.notes: Three lines added to page.tsx, plus the required workflow log file. No extraneous changes, no refactoring.

code_quality.notes: The footer element itself is clean and readable. Tailwind classes (text-center, text-stone-500, text-sm, py-4) are appropriate and consistent with the existing project style. Naming and structure are fine.

risk.notes: Broken build is the highest possible regression — the entire page fails to compile. The log file is also entirely empty (no status, decisions, or test coverage notes), though that is a workflow artifact concern rather than a product risk.
